conversation_history.summary: Full onboarding completed based on 4 months of activity data. Key profile: eMTB primary sport with structured indoor cycling (power/HR), swimming becoming regular, indoor climbing. Goal is balanced fitness build - both endurance and intensity. Minor injuries: right shoulder on long rides, left elbow recovering. BP management important. Yoga is a near-daily morning anchor. No upcoming races.

important_notes: Minor injury/physical limitation (not limiting training). No upcoming races or events.

Athlete monitors blood pressure — factor this into intensity recommendations. Avoid sustained very high intensity without adequate recovery. Aerobic/Zone 2 work is particularly valuable for BP management.
Right shoulder aches on long rides - monitor duration and position on bike. Left elbow took a hit but feels OK during training - watch for flare-ups after hard efforts or crashes.
FTP = 160W (eMTB isolated power meter, motor-excluded). Manually set estimate — schedule a proper FTP test around mid-April 2026 after current build block.

Winter-to-spring transition block plan agreed: ~6-8 weeks (Feb-April). Outdoor season starts April. Hard days: Mon/Wed/Sat. Swim 2x/week committed. Yoga daily morning. 1x bodyweight strength per week added. 1x hard indoor intervals mid-week (Wednesday). Semi-structured weekly template. Priority order: eMTB endurance > intensity > recovery > swimming > strength.
Week of Feb 23: Client visit all week, training will be minimal or zero. Treat as enforced recovery week. Transition block template starts week of March 2.
Athlete swims breaststroke, not freestyle. All swim workout recommendations must be tailored to breaststroke technique and pacing.
VO2 max: 51 ml/kg/min (from Garmin Connect, Feb 2026). Good baseline for intermediate endurance athlete.
Week of Feb 28 - Mar 5: Skiing holiday. No structured training. Counts as active recovery. Transition block template pushed to week of March 9.
Always use metric system (km, kg, celsius, meters). Week starts on Monday. Never use imperial units.
